that is, until @pipuffin graciously gave me his old guitar. i was previously working with a kids guitar from when my older brother was in, like, elementary school LOL

now i have a guitar with a much clearer tone and therefore my shoddy technique is more easily exposed hahaha

text: its fun bc even tho he’s a much better guitarist(?) than i am, we can both see our progress over continued long-term effort

raw.full_text: its fun bc even tho he’s a much better guitarist(?) than i am, we can both see our progress over continued long-term effort

text: its interesting that to make it sound the best that i can, upgrading the instrument helps but i also need to upgrade my own subtle tactile familiarities. but im frustrated that i need to relearn how to make a barre chord sound good after i thought i had it down pat!

raw.full_text: its interesting that to make it sound the best that i can, upgrading the instrument helps but i also need to upgrade my own subtle tactile familiarities. but im frustrated that i need to relearn how to make a barre chord sound good after i thought i had it down pat!

text: but also this whole (continuing!) experience is fun bc i forgot how much i missed the feeling of spot practicing something — not until i get it right but until i can’t get it wrong (as my old band director would say)

raw.full_text: but also this whole (continuing!) experience is fun bc i forgot how much i missed the feeling of spot practicing something — not until i get it right but until i can’t get it wrong (as my old band director would say)

text: but the logical end result here is that i put myself out of focus. it means i allow for others to have a much wider range of how to view me than i would myself. this is always a possibility but i think i treated it more as an inevitability (and inevitabilities are not actionable)

raw.full_text: but the logical end result here is that i put myself out of focus. it means i allow for others to have a much wider range of how to view me than i would myself. this is always a possibility but i think i treated it more as an inevitability (and inevitabilities are not actionable)

text: one place in particular ive noticed is when trying to prepare for the worst-faith interpretation of what im trying to say. bc in my head, a “reasonable” person may misinterpret what im saying

text: but if i have a clearer idea of who i am and what i stand for then i can draw a line. i dont have to concern myself with bad-faith interpretations bc thats not smth i would ever say or mean. anyone who would come away w that is no longer in the “reasonable person” camp

raw.full_text: but if i have a clearer idea of who i am and what i stand for then i can draw a line. i dont have to concern myself with bad-faith interpretations bc thats not smth i would ever say or mean. anyone who would come away w that is no longer in the “reasonable person” camp

text:
okay let me scratchpad some stuff here, bear with me

as with all(?) dichotomies, there’s also a frame where this split is just two sides of the same coin

society is built bottom-up from the individuals in it, and individuals are shaped by the society they live in

the scales only get tilted where we put our finger on it, how much importance (or the inverse, how much forgetting) we put on either side

the magic (and horror) of our current age is in the wide scale of impact/influence we receive, but if we forget we can exert that same impact outwards, there becomes a pressure differential. at an individual level, the (internal) generative force pales in comparison to the (external) collapsing force

in short, the problem of modernity is how the scale of our machinery exceeds our human scale. we build further than how far we can feel. very roughly, the “game” between the left and right is in disagreeing on whether we should attempt to feel further, or whether its not in human nature to do so

text: a friend i’ve recently made in nyc has been extremely supportive (honestly, more support than i need LOL). she’s been messaging me everyday congratulating me on being sober for X days. i understand the gravity she’s placing on this though. she mentioned that she’s always been sober bc of a family history of drinking and other substances, and that she’s been exposed to it since childhood. i’m lucky i grew up in better circumstances. but i suppose this is one of my takeaways — there is truly a dark side (even if i haven’t seen it personally) where it’s no longer casual fun, and actually causes generational harm

text:
perhaps a non-obvious phenomenon this diagram helps show is how “allowing people to treat you poorly” actually can land as an insult to the other person 

let’s say i’m Alice, and for whatever reason i am accustomed to people treating me poorly, and i have internalized that about myself. visually this means the purple blob in my head, which i believe to be a mostly accurate representation of my true self, is actually much more negative than it needs to be. 

Bob, from the outside, sees that this purple blob is super negative, and is unclear whether this negativity is limited to the purple blob itself, or whether it’s inherited from the superset blue blob (“Alice’s model of Bob”). in fact if Bob were unaware of the extent of Alice’s self-negativity, the simplest explanation in his head would be to assume that Alice views him as a bad person

ofc as more details come out as their relationship develops, Bob would likely come to understand the true apportionment of Alice’s negativity (directed inward, not outward). but if it were based only on first impressions, Bob likely wouldn’t want to be friends with Alice anyway, and Alice’s self-negativity might continue to spiral in a feedback loop :(

maybe im missing smth very simple here but the following should be internally consistent:

1. things are worse than they ever have been
2. #1 has always* been true

this keeps the importance of present-day issues as unique in their “objective” severity while also recognizing problems of this “relative” scale were able to be solved before, with whatever relative means we had

text: it is a silly (though necessary) dance to lend the appropriate gravity to someone’s concerns; at a first degree approx you’d like to match them exactly, since both undershooting and overshooting carries their own (possibly asymmetric) downsides; at a second degree you may realize once knowing someone well when they tend to under/overstress the situation (relative to what may be healthy for them — i’d argue this might also correlate with avoidant/anxious behavior, respectively) and consider both whether it is your responsibility to nudge someone one way or another, and how you could do so skillfully

i found a $2 bill when unloading the dryer; i’m reasonably certain it wasn’t in any of my pockets (when was the last time i handled a $2 bill? i’d think i would remember it); i’m also sure it wasn’t in the dryer to begin with either; i’ve exhausted all my reasonable possibilities which supposedly means whatever is left over, no matter how improbable, is the truth (i don’t believe this lemma from sherlock holmes because there are always near-infinite remaining possibilities); regardless, at the moment i am left to conclude that someone broke into my dryer at the laundromat to leave me a $2 bill; maybe someone was feeling particularly generous, though this strikes me odd; maybe it is futile to make sense of their inner machinations; maybe i should just chalk it up to laundromat luck
anyway, dinner is on me tonight 😝

text: I was 16 with my dad in the passenger seat, maybe the third or fourth time I was behind the wheel; this time I had to cross a major intersection with a stoplight; now obviously I knew red is stop and green is go and left is brake and right is gas; my problem was, how do I know that everybody else knows this? How can I trust that, when it's my turn to go, everybody on the other road would come to a halt? that fear dissipated after one or two crosses at the intersection, but the logic of it still holds to me; in this and in many other ways, I feel traffic is just a form of communication

text: this is an interesting topic that unfortunately rests on top of a sea of land mines, my take is that the broader goal is to avoid power disparity in relationships bc that power gap tends to distort romantic love (think of like a boss and subordinate). it becomes predatory, almost vampiric, when the one in power is “using” the powerless / exploiting their vulnerabilities. i do think it is possible for love to transcend that gap but it takes a certain kind of person (moral?). as a society we don’t really have faith in the other to “do what’s best”, or faith in ourselves to judge case-by-case on these situations, so it’s much easier to just stick to A Rule, and the line’s gotta be drawn somewhere. the decision to draw this threshold function taking in ages x and y and outputting Y/N on whether the rltp is acceptable inherently strips all context, as if everyone at the same age x or y are identical. i guess what im circling around is that we agree to A Rule, however arbitrary, bc it is preferable to a society with gray area here

text:
im not entirely sure if this exactly addresses what visa is looking for, but ill take this as a chance to share an odd, minor, and trivial story that still resonates w me wrt re-defining smth as artistic

usually when this happens for me its smth that i previously viewed only through a frame of “practicality”, until some invisible barrier was broken down that reminded me i can see it through aesthetics as well

my canonical example was in fashion. for the longest time i thought clothes were just fabric to cover up your body. at some point i decided to be a little bit more intentional, investing in new clothes + trying to match etc etc. i remember being excited to show my friend. she told me it looked good, but it didn’t match the bright red backpack i had on.

my immediate reaction was like “wait, wait, wait… that doesn’t count! the backpack is just a backpack, it’s holding my laptop, it’s not part of the fit! just ignore that part”

but my follow-up reaction was like “ahhh i was missing part of the picture, my frame was too narrow, fashion is about the whole image im presenting. the bag is more than just a container”

this practicality —> aesthetics barrier breakdown has happened to me in other areas since, and im sure will continue to happen even revisiting the same domain

text:
There’s a class of people who judge others based on certain characteristics, and expect your judgments to follow suit exactly. They will get up in arms over what kinds of characteristics should *determine* your decision rather than *influence* it. And in the inverse, they may steadfastly hold that some other characteristic should not determine *anything* (i.e. should be treated as inadmissible), when it may actually influence your gut judgment. 

And truthfully I don’t think this is a certain group of people alone. I think everyone has their own individual lines in the sand that they treat as hard boundaries (and they should!). I think the broader issue is whether those lines are something they arrive at individually, rife with nuance and complexity, or something they inherit from choosing to align with a broader group, which tend to lie closer to cut-and-dry directives. There’s no sliding scale of judgment, no gradation of perceived injury. There’s no gray area, no buffer at all.

To reiterate, I’m not saying people shouldn’t carve the world into black-and-white. Ideally everyone should stand for what they believe! But ideally, no two people’s boundaries line up exactly the same. The superposition of everyone’s black-and-white regions are what form the gray area. My concern is in the top-down aligning of these regions.

text: I have seen so many examples of Good People today — specifically Good Men In Relationships—  and I feel a mix of emotions about it; by far the largest is awe and inspiration; then there is guilt for not already being that way (it feels weird to have to see first to then emulate rather than to just Be a Good Person, because that means I will always be “behind” in some sense); then there’s a bit of resentment that I didn’t have some of these characteristics modeled to me from my dad; then some sadness that no one treated my mom the way she should’ve been; then the inspiration again to be that better person for my future wife and kids; then the slight frustration of having nowhere presently to direct that energy. Anyway. All in all I am always very grateful to have people around me with characteristics I aspire towards

text: I remember in high school during spring break I would go meet my brother in college and here and there I would basically audit a class; one of his classes was Human Sexuality (more psychology than anything), and I remember the professor repeated the point throughout the lecture that effectively there is no difference between the genders; I think she also said wherever these differences exist they can be attributed to socialization; this has informed most of my priors in this area and I largely agree but lately I’ve been thinking a) I don’t know necessarily how true a sweeping generalization like this is, and b) I’m not entirely sure what purpose it serves to distinctly delineate what is inherent vs what is contextual (I have my guesses, but I can come back to that); because like in the case of LGBTQ identity it’s viewed on the left almost doctrinally as the way you’re born; I’m not saying I necessarily disagree or that it’s inconsistent or even that it needs to be consistent, but doesn’t it seem odd, or at least at odds? Maybe it’s bc LGBTQ identity is “up for debate” in the public sphere in a way that being a woman isn’t (I’m not talking about the definition of a woman — that very much is — but just the existence of women in the abstract), and so maybe it provides more concrete founding to claim LGBTQ identity exists ab initio (am I using that right?), independently of ever-shifting context. Or maybe it’s an effort to avoid the way that the “socialization” aspect for LGBTQ identity raises alarm bells. Like you’ll hear “they’re turning the kids gay!” but never “they’re turning the girls into women!”. Lol I actually had no idea where this train of thought was gonna go when it started

text: Imo it’s not so much carcinization as much as it is that apple emojis have a monopoly on meaning-making by virtue of apple’s popularity/status/etc. the crying emoji in the QT is originally supposed to mean, well, crying, but slowly the meaning morphed to smth like “I’m crying bc this is so funny”. If other platforms don’t have the same emoji to communicate that exact same meaning then they’re left behind, and so they’re forced to converge
